description_long: This structure is a tall, lattice-framed tower constructed primarily from dark metal beams arranged in a geometric, truss-like pattern. The base is wide and tapers upward, supporting a Y-shaped upper section with two prominent horizontal crossarms extending outward. At the ends of each crossarm, there are insulator stacks designed to suspend high-voltage power lines. The overall design is open and skeletal, allowing for minimal wind resistance and efficient support of electrical infrastructure. The surface appears metallic and industrial, with a matte finish and visible structural joints. No warning signage or decorative elements are present, emphasizing its utilitarian function in urban power distribution.
description: A tall, dark metal lattice tower with a Y-shaped upper section and two horizontal crossarms supporting insulator stacks for power lines. The structure is open, skeletal, and industrial in appearance.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the wide base, Y-shaped upper section, and both crossarms with insulator stacks clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow profile of the tower and the depth of the lattice framework, with crossarms seen edge-on.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the symmetrical Y-shaped structure and crossarms with insulators.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, showing the slim profile and truss construction.
attribute_arrangement_of_crossarms: There are two horizontal crossarms arranged in a single tier, forming a wide Y-shape at the top of the structure. The crossarms are symmetrically positioned and extend outward from the central support.
attribute_configuration_of_attached_powe: Power lines are attached in a parallel configuration, with two circuits supported by the crossarms and insulator stacks at each end, likely arranged for three-phase transmission.
